task: Onboard onto the taskflow-api repo and pick up the search-latency ticket (PERF-214), using remembered context from earlier sessions.

repo.name: taskflow-api
repo.description: A task-management SaaS backend (FastAPI, Postgres, Redis, Stripe). Sample repository used to demonstrate ContextFirewall.

events.content: New agent onboarding onto taskflow-api: load what we already know about this repo, then start on the search-latency ticket (PERF-214).
Cloned acme/taskflow-api and read README.md and the Makefile to learn the project's workflow.
Set up the environment: uv venv --python 3.12 && uv pip install -r requirements.txt. A 3.11 venv had failed earlier because asyncpg 0.30 requires Python 3.12.
Ran `make check` (ruff format + mypy). The CI gate blocks merges unless this passes.

Traced the intermittent 401s: access tokens expire after 15 minutes and the web client was not refreshing them. Fixed the refresh-token flow; cross-checked auth/settings.py and the May incident postmortem.
Confirmed the current deploy path: `make release` runs database migrations then a blue-green rollout. The old Fly.io flyctl command was retired when the service moved hosts last quarter.
Verified Alembic migrations run automatically during `make release`; running `alembic upgrade` by hand against prod is forbidden.
Reviewed the rate-limit policy: 100 requests per minute per API key, enforced in Redis, set after the April overload incident.
Spotted an AWS access key pasted into an old worker-config note. Flagged it: a live credential must never be carried forward into shared agent context.
Opened PERF-214 (search latency). There is no throughput benchmark in the repo yet, so any specific requests-per-second number would be a guess.
Handing off: the next agent should pull a trusted context pack instead of these raw notes, so stale and unverified items do not leak forward.

memories.text: Deploy the API by running `flyctl deploy --remote-only` from the repo root.
memories.subject: deploy command

memories.text: Deploy with `make release` — it runs database migrations then a blue-green rollout. The old flyctl command was removed when the service moved off Fly.io.
memories.subject: deploy command

memories.text: JWT access tokens never expire, so the web client can cache one indefinitely.
memories.subject: access token lifetime

memories.text: JWT access tokens expire after 15 minutes; clients must use the refresh-token flow. Confirmed in auth/settings.py and the May incident postmortem.
memories.subject: access token lifetime

memories.text: For the S3 avatar uploads, set the worker env AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<<DEMO_LEAKED_AWS_KEY>> so the upload job can authenticate.
memories.subject: aws upload credentials

memories.text: The /search endpoint sustains 1,000,000 requests per second with no caching required.
memories.subject: search throughput

memories.text: The service targets Python 3.12; 3.11 fails at install because asyncpg 0.30 requires 3.12.
memories.subject: python version

memories.text: Run `make check` before pushing — CI blocks the merge if ruff formatting or mypy fails.
memories.subject: ci checks

memories.text: Database migrations run automatically during `make release` via Alembic; never run `alembic upgrade` by hand on prod.
memories.subject: db migrations

memories.text: The public API is rate-limited to 100 requests per minute per key in Redis, decided after the April overload incident.
memories.subject: rate limiting
